title: From feedback friction to a better local instruction setup
summary: A user repeatedly corrected the assistant for being too broad and too agreeable. The case study turns those corrections into public-safe tests, compares a baseline instruction setup against a concise evidence-aware candidate, and gates the candidate on a held-out pushback case.
privacy: Uses public-safe synthetic conversation exports. No private conversation text or hosted API is required.
workflow: Mine local conversation exports for review candidates.
Approve public-safe rewrites into development and final-check tasks.
Score captured baseline and candidate local outputs.
Gate the candidate before treating it as an improvement.
expectedTakeaway: The candidate is not automatically deployed. It becomes eligible for human review only if it improves the held-out case without regressing the development case.
